Home / Freelance Rate Calculator

Freelance Rate Calculator

Calculate your ideal hourly, daily, and monthly rate based on your skill, experience level, and market location.

How to Use This Tool

1
Select your primary skill.
2
Choose your experience level.
3
Pick your target market.
4
Review your recommended hourly, daily, weekly, monthly, and yearly rates.
5
Scroll down to compare rates across different skills and experience levels.
6
tools.solopreneur-freelance-rate-calculator.how_to_use.5

Frequently Asked Questions

How do I know if my rate is too high?

If you win every proposal, your rate is too low. Aim to lose 20-30% of proposals on price — that means you are charging what you are worth.

Should I charge different rates for different clients?

Yes. Charge startups less (with equity upside), enterprises more. Adjust for project complexity, deadline pressure, and ongoing relationship value.

How often should I raise my rates?

Every 6 months or after every 3 completed projects. Notify existing clients 30 days in advance. New clients get the new rate immediately.

What about value-based pricing?

For projects where you can quantify the client's ROI (e.g., 'this landing page will generate $50K'), charge 10-20% of the expected value instead of hourly.

Are these rates realistic for Asian markets?

The calculator adjusts for regional markets. However, if you work remotely for US/EU clients, charge US/EU rates regardless of where you live.

Results

Copy All
#1 💰 Freelance Rate Breakdown 🧠 Skill: Developer 📊 Experience: Mid-Level (3-5 years) 🌍 Market: US Market ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ ⏱️ Hourly Rate: $75/hr 📅 Daily Rate: $600/day 📆 Weekly Rate: $3,000/wk 📊 Monthly Rate: $12,990/mo 💰 Yearly Rate: $155,880/yr ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━ 💡 Pricing Tips: • Charge by the project, not by the hour. Clients buy outcomes, not time. • Double your hourly rate for rush jobs or weekend work. • Offer retainer packages (10/20/40 hrs per month) for steady income. • Raise rates every 6 months as you gain testimonials and portfolio pieces. • Your effective hourly rate = (project fee) / (actual hours). Track this. 📌 Formula: Base Rate ($75) × Experience (1x) × Location (1x)
#2 Comparison: Designer (junior) → $39/hr | $312/day | $6,752/mo
#3 Comparison: Writer (mid) → $50/hr | $400/day | $8,660/mo
#4 Comparison: Marketer (senior) → $96/hr | $768/day | $16,627/mo
#5 Comparison: Consultant (expert) → $250/hr | $2,000/day | $43,300/mo
